description: Apply a targeted fix to an existing Jinja2+Spintax template in the partner's local template repo and verify it with a test render against the engine. Frame collects context (repo path, fix description, branch). Build loads the four corthography-template-* standards skills as context, applies the edit, and pushes a feature branch via the partner's git auth. Evaluate runs corthography query+render+publish against --ref <branch> in the test environment. Promotion to prod is out of scope — once verified, the partner merges the branch and uses the template-publish workflow.

frame.description: Collect partner-repo path, fix description, branch name. Refuse to proceed on a dirty working tree.

steps.name: Collect hotfix context
steps.description: Prompt the partner for the absolute path to their local clone of the partner template repo, a one-line description of the fix, and the branch name to use. Verify the path is a git repo on a clean working tree before proceeding.
steps.prompt: Prompt the partner (in chat) for: (1) the absolute path to their local clone of the partner template repo for owner from {asset} — if they don't have one, surface the partner-repo URL from press-core's registry and tell them to clone first; (2) a one-line description of the fix; (3) a branch name (default: hotfix/<slug-of-description>-<YYYYMMDD>). Then verify the working tree is clean: run `git -C <path> status --porcelain` via Bash and confirm output is empty. If non-empty, stop the workflow with a clear message ('partner repo has uncommitted changes; commit or stash before running template-hotfix'). Also run `git -C <path> rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD` and remember the current branch so we can preserve context. Save the path, description, branch, and original-branch as workflow state for downstream steps.

steps.name: Create or checkout the hotfix branch
steps.description: Switch the partner's local repo onto the hotfix branch — create it if new, check it out if it already exists.
steps.prompt: Use Bash to switch the partner repo at <path> onto the hotfix branch. Try `git -C <path> switch -c <branch>` first; if that fails because the branch already exists, run `git -C <path> switch <branch>` instead. Confirm the active branch with `git -C <path> rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD` and report it.

build.description: Load the partner-facing template standards as context, apply the edit, ask for partner go-ahead, then commit and push.

steps.name: Load template standards as context
steps.description: Load the four corthography-template-* skills so the standards are available as context for the edit.
steps.prompt: Load all four corthography-template-* standards skills as context for the upcoming edit: corthography-template-spintax, corthography-template-sectioning, corthography-template-fields, corthography-template-config. Read each SKILL.md body fully. Briefly summarize back to the partner which of the four are most relevant to their stated fix description from frame-collect-context, and note any of the validation checklists that this fix should satisfy. The standards override any priors the model may have about template authoring — when in doubt, defer to the skill content.

steps.name: Apply the template fix
steps.description: Resolve the template path inside the partner repo (flatter layout — no owner tier — per partner-repo-standards), open index.md.j2 (and config.json if needed), apply the fix per the partner's description while honoring the standards loaded above, and surface the diff for partner review before commit.
steps.prompt: Resolve the template directory inside the partner repo: from {asset}, take the part before '+' as the template path. The partner repo uses a FLATTER layout than press-core — drop the leading owner tier, so the in-repo path is `<partner-repo-path>/templates/{collection}/{type}/{name}/`. Read index.md.j2 (always present) and config.json (always present). Apply the fix per the description from frame-collect-context, making ONLY the change required — do not refactor surrounding code. While editing, actively consult the standards skills loaded in build-load-standards: gate sections per corthography-template-sectioning, only reference fields per corthography-template-fields, keep Spintax deterministic per corthography-template-spintax, update config.json per corthography-template-config if endpoints/sample_items/item_explode are involved. After applying, show the partner the unified diff (run `git -C <path> diff` via Bash and surface the output) and ask them to confirm before commit. WAIT for explicit go-ahead — do not proceed to build-commit-push automatically.

steps.name: Commit and push the hotfix branch
steps.description: Commit the staged change with a conventional 'hotfix:' message and push the branch to origin using the partner's own git credentials.
steps.prompt: Use Bash to commit and push: `git -C <path> add <changed-files>` then `git -C <path> commit -m 'hotfix: <description-from-frame>'` then `git -C <path> push -u origin <branch>`. Report the resulting commit SHA and the remote branch URL. If the push fails (auth, network, divergent remote), surface the error verbatim and stop — the partner needs to resolve auth or pull and retry. Authentication uses the partner's own git credentials, NOT any press-core credentials.

evaluate.description: Run corthography query, render, and publish against --ref <branch> in the test environment to verify the fix end-to-end.

steps.name: Query (test, --ref hotfix-branch)
steps.description: Pull fresh data into the test environment, with the engine cloning the partner repo at the hotfix branch ref.
steps.prompt: Use the corthography-press-query skill to start a template-query run for target {asset} with --ref <branch> --env test --wait. The --ref flag tells the engine to clone the partner template repo at the hotfix branch before resolving the template. Report the run_id and final status. If the run fails before render even starts, that's a config.json or list_endpoint problem — surface the error and stop.

steps.name: Render (test, --ref hotfix-branch)
steps.description: Render the just-queried data through the edited template at the hotfix ref.
steps.prompt: Use the corthography-press-render skill to start a template-render run for target {asset} with --ref <branch> --env test --wait. Report the run_id and final status. If the render fails, the most common causes are: (1) Jinja2 syntax error introduced by the edit, (2) reference to a field the API doesn't actually return (see corthography-template-fields), (3) Spintax bracket imbalance. Surface the engine's error message verbatim — do not retry.

steps.name: Publish to test (--ref hotfix-branch)
steps.description: Push the rendered output to the test destination so the partner can eyeball the change at the test URL before deciding to merge.
steps.prompt: Use the corthography-press-publish skill to start a template-publish run for target {asset} with --env test --wait. Report the run_id and the test destination URL from the response. After this completes, surface a closing message to the partner: (1) all three test runs succeeded; (2) the change is visible at the test destination; (3) when they're satisfied, they should merge the hotfix branch into the partner repo's default branch and use the template-publish workflow (or the composite) to ship to prod — promotion to prod is intentionally NOT part of template-hotfix to avoid ambiguity about which ref ships.

autonomy.description: Frame and evaluate run autonomously. Build pauses inline (in chat, not via FABER approval gate) for the partner to review the diff before commit-push. Promotion to prod is intentionally a separate workflow.
